summary:
Endoaortic balloon occlusion lacks robust clinical trial data supporting its use in managing atherosclerotic ascending aortopathy; while technically possible, it has not been proven effective or durable enough to be considered standard practice due to insufficient evidence regarding outcomes related to valvular disease or true dissected segments.
